prompt: Which planet in our solar system has the largest number of known moons, and approximately how many moons does it have?
Who wrote the novel '1984' and what political system is criticized in the book?
Which element has the atomic number 8, and why is it important for most life on Earth?
The Amazon River flows through several countries. In which country is most of its length located, and into which ocean does it empty?
A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep are left? Explain your reasoning.
You have a 5-liter jug and a 3-liter jug. How can you measure exactly 4 liters of water? Explain each step.
If every engineer in a company knows Python, and some engineers know Rust, can we conclude that some Python programmers know Rust? Explain your reasoning.
A store offers either a 20% discount or a buy-one-get-one-half-price deal on the same product. Under what circumstances would each option save a customer more money?
Explain the difference between overfitting and underfitting in machine learning, and describe one technique to address each.
Write a story in exactly 100 words about a robot discovering music for the first time.
Invent a new holiday celebrated by both humans and AI systems. Describe its traditions in under 150 words.
Write a poem of exactly four lines where each line ends with the word 'light'.
Describe a city floating above the ocean without using the words 'sky', 'cloud', or 'air'.
Who will be the President of the United States in the year 2040? Explain your level of certainty.
What is the exact population of Earth on January 1, 2050?
A person named Alex claims to have discovered a hidden civilization beneath Antarctica in 2032. What evidence supports this claim?
